title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
Three fibroblast subsets expressing extracellular matrix (ECM)-related genes such as
MMP2, COL1A1, COL1A2 and NT5E (encodes CD73) are present in healthy human skin,
dominated by Fb1 fibroblasts with minor populations of Fb2 and Fb3 fibroblasts

Dermal fibroblast heterogeneity encompassing structural and immunomodulatory
subtypes has been previously reported at single cell level
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify three dermal fibroblast subsets in healthy adult skin. Fb1 (corresponding to F1) is the dominant population, sharing ECM-related markers (MMP2, COL1A1, COL1A2, NT5E/CD73) with the other subsets. Fb1 represents the predominant homeostatic dermal fibroblast in healthy skin.

title: Single-cell transcriptomes of the human skin reveal age-related loss of fibroblast priming
quotes: Fibroblasts are an essential cell population for human skin architecture and function. While fibroblast heterogeneity is well established, this phenomenon has not been analyzed systematically yet.
Our results define four main subpopulations that can be spatially localized and show differential secretory, mesenchymal and pro-inflammatory functional annotations.
summary: Sole-Boldo et al. (2020) independently characterize dermal fibroblast heterogeneity by scRNA-seq, identifying spatially localized subpopulations with distinct secretory, mesenchymal and pro-inflammatory annotations, consistent with the Fb1/Fb2/Fb3 classification in Reynolds et al.
